title: mikania badiniana
intro: Mikania badiniana, also known as Mexican root or wild angelica, is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating pain, making it a valuable remedy in folk medicine. Mikania badiniana is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used as a topical application to take advantage of its antiseptic properties.
